NOTIFICATION
Sub: Guidelines for Faculty/Program Advisors and Credits Limit
Ref: Ref: Notification No. IITJMU/Acad/2024/1036 dated 29 Oct 2024 (Link)
The competent authority has approved the following Guidelines for Faculty/Program Advisors.
This notification supersedes the previous notifications.
Background
The existing credit limit policy has been in effect for the past four semesters. Observations indicate
that the policy offers limited flexibility, which in some cases has led to:
• Accumulation of backlogs
• Pending courses
To address these challenge

metadata.title: Guidelines For Facultyprogram Advisors And Credits Limit

text: <!-- Page 2 --> 6. Students need to be counselled and convinced about the maximum allowable registration credit limit and ensure that the students perform well with suggested course load (more details are given below for backlog students). 7. Students are expected to adhere to all deadlines related to course add/drop. FAs/PAs will support students by reviewing the genuineness of requests and guiding them accordingly, while ensuring that all add/drop/credit-to-audit/withdrawal requests are completed within the prescribed timelines. 8. FAs/PAs are encouraged to guide students to maintain regular attendance during the semester to ensure continuity in their learning. In situations of genuine need, students may be advised to take only the essential leave, while also ensuring that the minimum attendance requirements are fulfilled. 9. In general, a student is expected to register to normal credits. The FA/PA can take a decision based on the difficulty level of the backlog courses opted and th

metadata.title: Guidelines For Facultyprogram Advisors And Credits Limit

text: However, students must maintain a CGPA of 7.0 or above to be eligible for the award of Minor or Specialization degrees at the time of graduation. Further, students should not have active backlogs to be eligible to register for minor/specialization courses. C. Moderated/Reduced course load 1. Reduced course load: Students may be recommended for a moderated/reduced course load based on backlogs, upon recommendation of the Faculty Advisor or Programme Advisor. 2. Probation phase: Students with four or more backlogs will be placed on probation; the FA/PA may suggest a reduced course load to allow slower-paced progression. 3. Degree completion: B.Tech. students are expected to complete the program in 8 semesters. Those unable to finish within 14 semesters (including semester drops) will be offered an exit option; no termination will occur solely due to backlogs.

The FA/PA shall coordinate with the HoD and DUGC to understand the credit requirement for
each semester (curriculum suggested semester wise course load) and the course load
shall be moderated as given below.
E. Preference of courses for controlled courses:
To reduce the course load, the OE courses shall be the first preference to be dropped, followed by
PE, then PC/DP courses, and then IC courses (with the exception of when a PC becomes a
prerequisite for other PCs to be offered in the immediately following semester). Backlog courses
are always given the last preference in case of dropping the courses. Here again, there can be an
exception, if the credit limits force the dropping of a PC that can be a prerequisite thereafter for
several PC courses in the next semester. FA/PA shall duly verify the prerequisites for future
courses and make a decision prior

# Guidelines for Preparatory Course Students

metadata.title: Guidelines For Preparatory Course Students

text: <!-- Page 1 --> Guidelines for Preparatory Course Students Preparatory course is starting at IIT Jammu in AY 2023-24 . 1. Preparatory course is a pre-degree course and admission to the actual degree program is conditional upon successful completion of the Preparatory course. 2. These Rules are applicable to students of one year (2 semesters) Preparatory course and shall be applicable from the AY 2023-24 3. Only after successful completion of Preparatory Courses, students can pursue the program they are admitted for at the earmarked IIT. Also, the stream for your regular B.Tech. Degree programme will be the same as it is decided by JOSAA (in the year of admission to Preparatory course). 4. Each session is divided into 2 semesters each: Autumn semester I (August to December) and Spring Semester II (January to June). The academic activities for a season will be laid down in the Academic Calendar for the session. 5. End- Semester examination is the final examination at the end of each seme

metadata.title: Guidelines For Preparatory Course Students

text: pass in all subjects by securing at least 35% marks in each subject. 9. For students failing only in one subject, grace marks upto 5 % may be given by the instructor. 10. Evaluation will include Homework, Assignments, Class Participation, Quizzes, Mid Semester and End Semester Examinations. The weightage of each of these components will be informed by the Instructors. 11. No Degree / Diploma certificate is issued after successful completion of Preparatory Courses. 12. All other rules such as attendance requirement, punishment for adoption of unfair means in Examination, rules governing maintenance of discipline etc. shall be governed by the rules given in Rules and Regulations of UG 2022 batch. # Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

**Notification No.:** IITJMU/Acad/2024/1268  
**Date:** 01 April 2024

# Guidelines for Mini-Project

## Notification

The Competent Authority has approved the following guidelines for the **Mini-Project** course.

Mini Project in the **2022 UG Curriculum** is a **1.5-credit Design Core course**. Design Core courses are introduced to provide knowledge related to practical and significant aspects of engineering and sciences at a basic level.

In the **20th Senate**, the Mini Project was made mandatory for UG students of all branches and specializations following the 2022 curriculum.

The genesis of the Mini Project arises from th

metadata.title: Guidelines For Mini Project

text:
## Student Registration and Project Selection

- Each department can host a maximum of **60 UG students** under the Mini Project course.
- Before the last date of registration, students may interact with faculty members to identify and finalize a suitable Mini Project topic.
- Based on the selected topic, students will register for the corresponding Mini Project course during semester registration.

---

## Team Formation

### Group Size

Each project group shall consist of:

- Minimum: **2 students**
- Maximum: **4 students**

These limits are intended to encourage teamwork and collaborative learning.

### Interdisciplinary Teams

A project group may contain students from:

- The same department, or
- Multiple departments

working together on the same Mini Project topic.

---

# Evaluation Process

The Mini Project shall be evaluated by the department offering the course at the end of the semester.

The Course Coordinator (CC) shall submit the grades.

Departments may adopt **relative

metadata.title: Guidelines For Mini Project

text:
## Role of Course Coordinator

The Course Coordinator shall be a member of the evaluation committee for all project groups except:

- Those groups for which the Course Coordinator himself/herself serves as the Supervisor.

---

## Independence of Evaluation

The committee evaluation shall remain:

**Independent of the Supervisor's evaluation.**

Committee assessment and Supervisor assessment shall contribute separately according to the prescribed weightages.

---

# Applicability

These guidelines apply to:

**UG Students of 2022 and 2023 Batches**

following the 2022 curriculum.

---

# Copy To

1. Dean Academic Affairs
2. Associate Dean (UG)
3. All Faculty Members
4. Webmaster (for uploading on the Institute Website)

---

## Issuing Authority

**Assistant Registrar (Academic Affairs)**

**Indian Institute of Technology Jammu**

- Library
- Supervisor

before graduation.

### Book Purchase Procedure

1. Obtain a Non-Availability Certificate from the Library.
2. Supervisor requests purchase through the Library.
3. Library purchases and issues the book.

The student may retain the book during the PhD tenure but must return it before leaving the Institute.

### viii. Other Approved Expenditure

Any other expenditure approved by Dean Academic Affairs including:

- Registration charges
- National travel support
- Internationa

# Notification-Extension of HTRA to Female Scholars

# Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

# Extension of HTRA / Fellowship to Female PhD Scholars

## Purpose

The Institute may extend the stipend/fellowship for full-time female PhD research scholars beyond the normal fellowship period under exceptional circumstances and on justifiable grounds.

---

## Eligibility Conditions

The benefit may be granted only if all of the following conditions are satisfied:

### 1. Completion of Normal Fellowship Period

The extension shall be considered only after the scholar has availed:

- 60 monthly scholarships (5 years of fellowship)

and only upon recommendation of the Superviso

metadata.title: Notification-Extension Of Htra To Female Scholars

text:
### First Preference

Female scholars belonging to the:

- Economically Weaker Section (EWS)

shall be given preference.

### Further Tie-Breaking

If a tie still exists:

- Preference shall be based on the severity of circumstances,
- As decided by the Dean/Director.

---

## Key Takeaway

A female PhD scholar at IIT Jammu can receive fellowship support in the 6th year if:

- She has exhausted the normal 5-year fellowship period,
- Has not yet submitted her thesis,
- Has not completed the synopsis seminar,
- Has valid exceptional circumstances (illness, maternity, family medical issues, etc.),
- Receives SRC and Supervisor recommendation,
- Falls within the institute's 15% cap on such extensions.

text: <!-- Page 1 --> IITJMU/Acad/2024/1170 16 Dec, 2024 Notification Sub: Policy for Early Start PhD Program The 26th senate has approved the policy for Early Start PhD Program at IIT Jammu to encourage meritorious B.Tech students from reputed Institutions. The details of the policy are as mentioned below: 1. Introduction Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu is committed to fostering a world-class research environment that attracts talented and motivated individuals from diverse academic backgrounds. The Early Start PhD Program of IIT Jammu aims to provide opportunities to existing students of empanelled/reputed institutes for early engagement in research, offering flexible and alternative entry routes in contributing to cutting-edge research at IIT Jammu. The following guidelines outline the details and admission process: 2. Objectives The primary goal of this initiative is to identify and nurture young and potential students, and to inspire them in taking research in their early car

metadata.title: Notification-Policy For Early Start Phd Program

text: selection process. At least two faculty members from IIT Jammu will visit empanelled/reputed institutes in the months of June/July or October/November to conduct initial interviews and shortlist candidates for admissions under this scheme. Such shortlisted candidates will be evaluated by DPGC committee, and the candidates will be initially invited for a research position i.e. to do their B.Tech project including last semester’s course work on IIT Jammu campus. The students must pay only the hostel, amenity, and food charges for their stay on the campus. Such students will be receiving their undergraduate degree from their parent institutes. Interested candidates from these

metadata.title: Notification-Policy For Early Start Phd Program

text:
---

<!-- Page 2 -->
research positions will be asked to apply for PhD program in the 1st week of April in a parallel
advertisement along with regular PhD advertisement. All applicants will be evaluated by selection
committees of each department in April/May (i.e., end of 8th semester of student) before they leave to
their campuses and few candidates will be offered PhD admission, based on the performance in their
project, interview and availability of seats with the department.
4. Eligibility Criteria
Candidates applying must fulfil the following eligibility criteria:
For B.Tech Students:
• Minimum CPI/CGPA of 8.0 at the end of the third year for General/EWS/OBC candidates, or 7.5
for SC/ST/PwD candidates.
• No GATE qualification is required for these candidates.
• The candidates should not have any active backlog course at the time of final interview.
• Admission requires candidates to provide official documentation confirming the successful
completion of their UG degree.
5. Other Co

metadata.title: Notification-Policy For Early Start Phd Program

text:
<!-- Page 3 -->
contingency grants to support research activities, purchase of research materials, and travel to
conferences as per laid norms by IIT Jammu.
6.2. Fellowship Duration
At present, the maximum period for which IIT Jammu provides scholarship to PhD candidates is
typically 5 years.
6.2. Quota
At present, each department will be given one early start PhD student per year. This shall be within the
maximum limit of 8 for a faculty to supervise PhD students as main supervisor including all categories
(Institute funding, project/external funding, JDP, off-campus etc)
7. Empanelment process
7.1 Each department will be asked to provide a list of empanelled institutes in their specialization, which
will be approved by the Senate Chairman. The process of empanelment is:
a) Invitation by HoD to all faculty members to submit a list of their known reputed colleges/ universities
(excluding IITs, NITs, IISERs).
b) A DFB will be called by the HoD to discuss the list and shortlist not more

text: <!-- Page 1 --> No. IITJMU/Acad/2024/1035 29 October 2024 Notification Sub: Six-month internship policy for full time regular B.Tech and PG Students The 26th Senate has approved the Six Month internship for Fulltime regular B.Tech and PG students. The details of the policy are as mentioned below: Policy: 1. Fulltime regular UG & PG students of all disciplines may avail long term internships up to 6- month duration within the window mentioned below: a. B.Tech Students in 8th Semester (December - June) b. M. Tech/M.Sc. student 4th Semester (December - June) c. M. Tech /MS(R)(3 Years) 6th Semester (December- June) The above internship for B.Tech students is over and above the summer internship of 1 credit. For PG students, internship is not part of curriculum, and no credits can be claimed. 2. Students can avail up to 6-month internships in either one of the following types of organizations: a. An Industry b. A Research Lab c. Academic Institutes (The academic institutions for such an int

metadata.title: Notification - Six-Month Internship Policy For Full Time Regular B.Tech And Pg Students

text: year onwards to proceed for internship in the last semester under the guidance of the faculty advisor and DUGC. 6. Postgraduate students should begin planning in their 2nd semester to pursue an internship in the final semester, under the guidance of the program advisor, supervisor and the DPGC. PG students can proceed with the approvals only after allocation of MTP supervisor. Internship during 3rd semester is not allowed. 7. Once a B. Tech student secures an internship opportunity, they must obtain approval from the DUGC, as well as from the Program advise, BTP supervisor, AD UG, Dean AA and the PIC- CDS. 8. Once an M. Tech/M.Sc./MS(R) student secures an internship opportunity, they must obtain

metadata.title: Notification - Six-Month Internship Policy For Full Time Regular B.Tech And Pg Students

text:
<!-- Page 2 -->
approval from the Supervisor, DPGC Chairperson, AD-PG, Dean AA, and PIC-CDS.
9. M.Tech students shall complete all the coursework before going for a long-term internship and
online courses are not allowed. The supervisor and the DPGC will decide whether MTP is to be
completed online or offline. If offline mode is finalized, then the student can only opt for online
internships.
10. The student will have to report to the campus after completing the internship and must complete
his/her BTP/Dissertation work.
11. For all students, the presentation and exam of the BTP/MTP will be in offline mode.
12. No stipend or scholarship will be provided to students during their internship, regardless of
whether they receive payment from external organizations or not. PG students are not allowed
to used contingency for travel for or during internships.
13. The Career Development Services (CDS) will process such requests and handle the necessary
documentation. No long-term internships wi

# Notification 7.4

# Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

**Notification No.:** IITJMU/Acad/2025/721  
**Date:** 04 July 2025

# Notification

## Subject

### Modification of R 7.4 of PhD Ordinance: Guidelines for Appointment of Supervisors/Joint-Supervisors and Caretaker-Supervisor for Ph.D. Students

In the event that a PhD supervisor exits IIT Jammu due to:

- Resignation (with or without lien)
- Termination
- Death
- Long leave
- Deputation
- Extended research visits exceeding 12 months
- Medical conditions resulting in long or indeterminate recovery periods (more than 12 months)

the following guidelines shall be applicable for the transfer of PhD students assigned to that supervisor.

---

## General Provision

These provisions

metadata.title: Notification 7.4

text:
### Note

This stage generally refers to students who:

- Have already spent substantial time on their research topic.
- Have completed part of their research objectives.

### Guidelines

1. A new main supervisor shall be appointed by:

   - DPGC
   - HoD

   in consultation with the outgoing supervisor, preferably aligning with the student's research topic.

2. Appointment of the new supervisor must be completed before the outgoing faculty member applies for No-Dues clearance.

3. The newly appointed supervisor may serve as the sole supervisor.

4. If supervision quota is unavailable:

   - The student shall be treated as supernumerary.
   - The allocation shall be adjusted against a regular quota when a vacancy becomes available.

5. The outgoing faculty member may continue as a co-supervisor, subject to:

   - Submission of a No Objection Certificate (NoC) from the new organization/institute.
   - Consent of the newly appointed main supervisor.

6. If expertise is unavailable within

metadata.title: Notification 7.4

text:
### Recommendation

It is strongly recommended that in future:

- Candidates should first be appointed as project staff.
- PhD admission should subsequently be offered.

This enables transfer of the student together with the project whenever necessary.

---

## Case 2: Comprehensive Examination Completed

### Guidelines

1. If the project is transferred:

   - The student shall be transferred to the new institute.

2. If the student wishes to continue at IIT Jammu:

   - Rules specified under **Section A2** shall apply.

3. If:

   - 18 months of PhD registration have been completed, and
   - 18 months of project tenure have been completed,

   the student may be transferred to Institute funding as per Section A2.

4. The outgoing supervisor may continue as co-supervisor by following provisions specified under Section A2.

---

## Case 3: Pre-Synopsis Completed

### Guidelines

1. Same provisions as specified under **Section A3** shall apply.

2. In addition, the outgoing faculty membe

# Notification Open Research Day Policy

<!-- Page 1 -->
# Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

# Open Research Day Policy

The Competent Authority has approved the **Open Research Day Policy** for UG, PG, and Ph.D. students of IIT Jammu.

---

# Objectives

The Open Research Day aims to:

- Showcase research activities being carried out at IIT Jammu.
- Provide a platform for UG, PG, and Ph.D. students to present their research work.
- Encourage interdisciplinary interaction among researchers.
- Promote collaborations between academia, research institutions, and industry.
- Increase awareness about ongoing research activities within the Institute.
- Attract prospective students and researchers

metadata.title: Notification Open Research Day Policy

text:
### External Participants

- Ph.D. Scholars and students from State Universities and CFTIs of Jammu & Kashmir (Invited)
- B.Sc./B.E./B.Tech./M.Sc. students from nearby engineering colleges, particularly:
  - Final-year students
  - Students interested in pursuing:
    - Ph.D.
    - M.Sc.
    - M.Tech.
      at IIT Jammu
- Industry professionals and representatives

---

# Benefits of the Event

The Open Research Day provides opportunities for:

- Visitors to interact with IIT Jammu students and researchers.
- Exploration of state-of-the-art research facilities at IIT Jammu.
- Understanding residential and academic facilities available at the Institute.
- Fostering interdisciplinary collaborations among faculty members.
- Increasing awareness of research being conducted across departments.
- Encouraging collaborations with:
  - Universities
  - Research institutions
  - Industries
  - Government organizations

The event also enables prospective employers to interact directly with studen

metadata.title: Notification Open Research Day Policy

text:
### Demonstrations

- Participants shall be provided dedicated space for demonstrations.
- Classrooms may be allocated for software demonstrations where required.

---

# Awards

The jury shall select the:

**Top 10 researchers**

Each selected researcher shall receive:

**₹30,000 Prize Money**

---

# Jury Composition

The jury shall consist of:

- Senior Professors of the Institute

provided that:

- Their students are not participating in the event.

This is intended to ensure fairness and impartial evaluation.

---

# Expected Outcomes

The Open Research Day is expected to:

- Increase visibility of IIT Jammu research.
- Encourage student participation in research activities.
- Strengthen industry-academia interactions.
- Promote collaborations across departments and institutions.
- Inspire prospective students to pursue advanced studies and research.
- Create networking opportunities for students, faculty, researchers, and industry professionals.

---

# Applicability

Applicable

# Notification SoP Institute Grade Moderation

<!-- Page 1 -->
IITJMU/Acad/2024/1055 11th November 2024
NOTIFICATION
Sub: SoP for Institute Grade Moderation
The Competent Authority has approved the following SoP for Institute Grade Moderation.
(A) The Grade Moderation data received from each department shall be reviewed by the Grade
Moderation Committee.
(i) FIC Grades and Registration shall conduct a statistical analysis of the grade distribution for each
course.
(ii) The average grade across all theory courses in the Institute for a given semester shall be
calculated by the FIC. Laboratory courses, dissertation, and project related courses may be omitted
from these calculations.

metadata.title: Notification  Sop Institute Grade Moderation

text:
<!-- Page 2 -->
(v) If the average grade in the course falls above 7.5 or below 6.5, the course coordinator must evaluate
whether this deviation reflects actual student performance, course difficulty, or an assessment
imbalance. Necessary steps to address such cases should be discussed in the department grade
moderation and reported to the Institute Grade Moderation committee.
(vi) In general, the cumulative grades for a particular course taught several times should follow normal
distribution.

# Notification

## Subject

### Procedure for Proposing and Introducing New M.Tech. Programs at IIT Jammu

The Competent Authority has approved a new mechanism for proposing and introducing new **M.Tech. programmes** at IIT Jammu.

IIT Jammu is growing with the addition of infrastructure such as:

- Offices
- Classrooms
- Laboratories
- Hostels

With the planned and measured expansion of institutional infrastructure, the growth of academic programmes must remain aligned with available resources.

Accordingly, the number of new programmes, including **M.Tech. and MS programmes**, shou

metadata.title: Notification  Academics

text:
## 7. Faculty Availability

The proposal must include details of faculty members from IIT Jammu who:

- Possess expertise in the proposed area
- Will own and teach programme courses

Departments must consider:

- Current UG teaching load
- Current PG teaching load
- Faculty availability

before proposing a new programme.

---

# Procedure

## Submission Timeline

Any proposal for a new programme to be offered from the next academic year must be submitted to:

**Associate Dean (PG)**

on or before:

**31 October of the previous academic year**

after due processing through the concerned **DPGC**.

---

## Discussion at SPGB

Proposals received from individual departments or collaborative groups shall be discussed in:

**SPGB (Senate Postgraduate Board)**

for possible recommendations.

---

## Prioritization of Programmes

Since only a limited number of new programmes can be introduced over a period of 3–4 years, the Chairman, Senate may seek recommendations from an external expert comm

metadata.title: Notification  Academics

text:
## Step 5: Incorporation of Suggestions

The Programme Coordinator and department shall incorporate modifications suggested by the reviewers.

The revised proposal shall then be submitted to:

**SPGB**

for further consideration.

---

## Step 6: Presentation before SPGB

The Programme Coordinator shall make a presentation before SPGB.

SPGB may:

- Recommend the proposal to the Senate through the Board of Academic Affairs Committee
- Return the proposal for modifications if necessary

---

## Step 7: Senate Recommendation and BoG Approval

If a large number of proposals are received:

- The Chairman, Senate may constitute a review committee.
- The committee shall prioritize the proposals.

A limited number of programmes from the priority list shall then be recommended by the Chairman, Senate to the:

**Board of Governors (BoG), IIT Jammu**

taking into consideration:

- Space availability
- Infrastructure
- Other institutional constraints

---

## 7. Ownership in Interdisciplinary Programmes

For interdisciplinary programmes:

Departments shall clearly demonstrate the:

from leading organizations.

Academic experts should include:

- Professors

from reputed institutions in India and abroad.

# Notification

## Subject

### Tuition Fee Waiver / Exemption – Income Category of Students and Documents Required Thereof

To protect the interests of socially and economically backward students, the following provisions are available regarding tuition fees charged by IITs.

---

# 1. Tuition Fee Waiver / Remission Policy

## A. SC / ST / PH Students

Students belonging to:

- SC
- ST
- PH (PwD)

shall receive:

**100% Tuition Fee Waiver**

irrespective of family income.

---

## B. Most Economically Backward Students

Students who

metadata.title: Tuition Fee Waiver Notification Ay 2026-27

text:
### Portal Request Type

Select:

```text
Income Certificate / Category Change Request
```

---

## Income Certificate Requirements

The income certificate must:

- Include income from **all family income sources**
- Correspond to Financial Year **2025–26**
- Be issued **on or after 01 April 2026**

---

# 4. Consequence of Non-Submission

If a student fails to submit the required documents before the deadline:

- The student shall be upgraded to the **highest income category** (Above ₹5 Lakh)
- No tuition fee remission shall be provided for Academic Year 2026–27

---

# 5. Students with Income Above ₹5 Lakh

Students belonging to:

- General
- OBC-NCL
- EWS

whose family income exceeds ₹5 lakh per annum:

- Need not submit income-related documents

unless they wish to seek a change in income category based on reduced family income during FY 2025–26.

---

# 6. SC / ST / PH Students

SC/ST/PH students:

- Need not submit income certificates
- Need not submit remission documents

metadata.title: Tuition Fee Waiver Notification Ay 2026-27

text:
### Digital Income Certificate

Where the state government issues online/digital income certificates:

**Digital Income Certificate is mandatory.**

If the state does not issue digital certificates, the prescribed Form C format shall be used.

---

### Acceptable Authority

Income Certificate must be signed on **all pages** by:

- First Class Magistrate
- SDO
- BDO
- Tehsildar

of the district of residence.

Certificates signed by:

- Notary
- Oath Commissioner

shall **not be accepted**.

---

# Form C (UG)

## Income Certificate Format

### Student Details

- Student Name
- Student ID
- Category

---

### Declaration

The parent/guardian declares the total annual family income from all sources including:

- Salary
- Agriculture
- Trading
- Pension
- Interest
- Investments
- Business Income
- Other Income Sources

including income of spouse and unmarried children.

---

## Income Breakup

### A. Parent / Guardian Income

1. Salary / Profession / Pension
2. Business Income
3. Agricultu

metadata.title: Tuition Fee Waiver Notification Ay 2026-27

text:
### Attestation

The certificate must be:

- Sworn before the competent authority
- Signed
- Sealed with date

by:

- First Class Magistrate
- SDO
- BDO
- Tehsildar
- Equivalent Revenue Officer

---

# Self-Declaration Affidavit

## Requirements

Self-Declaration must be:

- On ₹10 Non-Judicial Stamp Paper
- Attested by a Notary

---

## Contents

The affidavit shall include:

1. Citizenship declaration
2. Parent/guardian details
3. Annual family income from all sources
4. Declaration regarding earning/non-earning family members

---

## Liability Clause

The student shall certify that:

- All information provided is true.
- Any false declaration may result in:
  - Recovery of financial assistance
  - Penal interest
  - Legal action
  - Disciplinary action

---

# Key Summary

| Family Income | Tuition Fee Benefit |
|---------------|--------------------|
| SC/ST/PH | 100% Tuition Fee Waiver |
| Less than ₹1 Lakh | 100% Tuition Fee Remission |
| ₹1 Lakh – ₹5 Lakh | 2/3 Tuition Fee Remis

id: academics:course:Course Outcomes (about 100 Words): After Successful Completion Of The Course, Students Are Expected To Understand The Fundamental Design Principles Of Various Kind Of Biosensors Available In The Market, Or Their Potential Use In The Developing State-of-the Art Instruments. Emphasis Would Be Given To The Active Participation Of Students, Hence Expected To Include Designing Term Projects And Presentation, Technical Writings Etc
text: Course:  After successful completion of the course, students are expected to understand the fundamental design principles of various kind of biosensors available in the market, or their potential use in the developing state-of-the art instruments. Emphasis would be given to the active participation of students, hence expected to include designing term projects and presentation, technical writings etc..

metadata.label: Course
metadata.name: After successful completion of the course, students are expected to understand the fundamental design principles of various kind of biosensors available in the market, or their potential use in the developing state-of-the art instruments. Emphasis would be given to the active participation of students, hence expected to include designing term projects and presentation, technical writings etc.

id: academics:course:Course Contents: (topics To Appear As Course Contents In The Courses Of Study Booklet) (include Practical / Practice Activities): A Brief History, Definitions, Biological Inspiration, Types Of Sensors, Target Analytes, Various Recognition Events, Bio Affinity: Labeled And Label Free, Whole Cell Sensing - Bacteria, Yeast, Mammalian Cell, Biomolecule Immobilization Techniques. Types Of Transduction Mechanism, Optical; Electrochemical And Mass Based Which Includes Colorimetric, Ecl, Surface Plasmon Resonance, Electro Chemical; Fet, Impedance, Piezoelectric; Cantilever, Fluorescence Calibration, Dynamic Range, Signal To Noise, Sensitivity, Selectivity, Interference. Micro Fabricated Biosensors And Point-of-care Diagnostics Systems, Noninvasive Biosensors In Clinical Analysis, Biosensors For Early Diagnostics, Disease Managements Such Its Use In Diabetes Management, Cancer Management And Other Diseases. State-of-art Technologies Used For Bio Sensing Such As Aptamer Based Recognition Units, Signal Enhanced Assays, Nanopore-based Sensors
text: Course:  (Topics to appear as course contents in the Courses of Study booklet) (Include Practical / Practice activities): A brief history, Definitions, biological inspiration, types of sensors, target analytes, various recognition events, Bio Affinity: Labeled and Label free, whole cell sensing - bacteria, yeast, mammalian cell, Biomolecule immobilization techniques. Types of Transduction mechanism, Optical; Electrochemical and Mass based which includes Colorimetric, ECL, Surface Plasmon Resonance, Electro chemical; FET, Impedance, Piezoelectric; Cantilever, fluorescence calibration, dynamic Range, signal to noise, sensitivity, selectivity, Interference. Micro fabricated biosensors and point-of-care diagnostics systems, Noninvasive biosensors in clinical analysis, Biosensors for early diagnostics, Disease Managements such its use in Diabetes management, cancer management and other diseases. State-of-art technologies used for bio sensing such as aptamer based recognition units, signal enhanced assays, nanopore-based sensors..

Constitution Of SRC is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Within 60 days of joining PhD programme. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.

Progress Evaluation is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Every semester. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.

Progress Report Submission is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Every semester till thesis submission. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.

Research Semester Evaluation is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Oral presentation before SRC. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.

Coursework Semester Evaluation is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Mandatory evaluation and report submission. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.

Report Length is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Maximum 20–25 pages. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.

Consequence Of Non-submission is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Registration for next semester may be denied. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.

Consequence Of Delayed SRC Formation is a member of the General (Implementation of rules regarding Student Research Committee (SRC) Composition and End Semester Evaluation - PhD Scholar) of Institute department at IIT Jammu. Role/Designation: Fellowship may not be released. Notification Date: 08 June 2023.
